title: Lateral Thinking
author: Edward de Bono
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A classic lateral-thinking puzzle: a 3x3 grid of dots. The 'outside the box' answer is achieved by an L-shaped hairline that exits the grid to the upper-right. The exit point is RED. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1280)" fill="none">
    <!-- A 3x3 grid of dots, 400px apart -->
    <g fill="#cccccc" stroke="none">
      <circle cx="-400" cy="-400" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="0" cy="-400" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="400" cy="-400" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="-400" cy="0" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="400" cy="0" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="-400" cy="400" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="0" cy="400" r="14"/>
      <circle cx="400" cy="400" r="14"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The implied box (the 3x3 grid's bounding edges) — faint -->
    <line x1="-400" y1="-400" x2="400" y2="-400" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.3"/>
    <line x1="-400" y1="400" x2="400" y2="400" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.3"/>
    <line x1="-400" y1="-400" x2="-400" y2="400" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.3"/>
    <line x1="400" y1="-400" x2="400" y2="400" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.3"/>
    <!-- The classic 'connect all 9 dots with 4 lines' solution: a 4-segment L-shaped path -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2.5">
      <line x1="-400" y1="-400" x2="400" y2="-400"/>
      <line x1="400" y1="-400" x2="400" y2="400"/>
      <line x1="400" y1="400" x2="-400" y2="400"/>
      <line x1="-400" y1="400" x2="-400" y2="-400"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The LATERAL solution: extends BEYOND the box, exits upper-right -->
    <line x1="-400" y1="400" x2="-400" y2="-700" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="-400" y1="-700" x2="700" y2="-700" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="700" y1="-700" x2="700" y2="-400" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="700" y1="-400" x2="400" y2="-400" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- A red arrow at the exit point pointing further out -->
    <line x1="700" y1="-700" x2="900" y2="-900" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.6"/>
    <polyline points="880 -900 900 -900 900 -880" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
    <!-- A label -->
    <text x="900" y="-920" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.2em">OUTSIDE</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Lateral thinking = exiting the box. A literal 3x3 grid of dots (the iconic 'nine-dot problem'). The graphite 'vertical-thinking' solution (4 lines around the box) is drawn in gray. The LATERAL solution in RED 3px: extends beyond the box, exits upper-right, with a small red arrow continuing outward to 'OUTSIDE'. The cover IS the central insight of the book. Title sits in the bottom 1/3, weight 300 whisper, mixed-case. Brand 'DE BONO' in the top-left; pill '↗' at the top — the lateral direction.
